rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 30, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Second Amended Complaint to be filed and served no later than October 30, 2023. No later than November 3, 2023 Plaintiff to file and calendar a Motion to Continue Trial in Department 206 and seek a trial continuance for a new trial no earlier than May 6, 2024. Parties are encouraged to meet and confer on a new trial date and seek ex-parte continuance based on stipulation. If Plaintiff fails to file a Motion to Continue Trial, defendants may appear ex-parte with a request to strike the Second Amended Complaint and to restore the Motions for Summary Judgment/Adjudication to calendar. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To His Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One, To Defendant Chito K. Saha; To Issue An Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Information And Documents Of Sahas Business Entities, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 30, 2023 line 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, TO DEFENDANT CHITO K. SAHA; TO ISSUE AN ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF FINANCIAL INFORMATION AND DOCUMENTS OF SAHA'S BUSINESS ENTITIES, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production is GRANTED. Documents must be produced no later than November 3, 2023. Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories are DENIED provided that verifications are sent no later than November 3, 2023. Request for sanctions is DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ROBERT Q HOANG VS. ANDERSEN, JUNG & CO., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 30, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly state that the moving defendant is not sued in his professional capacity. The allegations in the complaint, as currently made, render the complaint uncertain on this issue. Plaintiff shall further allege facts in support of each element of the general negligence cause of action against the moving defendant, including duty, breach and damages. Plaintiff shall also allege facts showing that the cause of action for negligence accrued within the applicable statute of limitations on November 17, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: LOREN MILLER HOMES VS. MICHAEL MINOR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 30, 2023 line 12. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:00 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The lease that Plaintiff submitted did not show a landlord-tenant relationship between the Plaintiff and Defendant. The proof of service for the 30-day notice indicates that the notice was delivered less than 30 days before performance was due. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
